calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Continued To Jul-01-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER LEICHUS AND MARLENE LEICHUS' MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is granted. Under the multiple-cause principle, Defendants are entitled to transfer the entire action to their place of residency, Placer County, because they are entitled to a change of venue for the fraud cause of action. See Jhirmack Enterprises, Inc. v. Superior Court (1979) 96 Cal.App.3d 715, 720-721. Tort-based actions for personal injury or death are triable either in the county where defendant resides or in the county where the injury occurred. But tort-based actions for injury to reputation, or even for mental or emotional distress, such as severe humiliation, anguish, emotional distress and trauma, are triable only at a defendant's residence. (Carruth v. Superior Court (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 215, 219-220; Cubic Corp. v. Superior Court (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 622, 625.) Plaintiff's fraud cause of action is triable only at Defendants' residence because it does not entail any physical injury or death. The request for fees is denied. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Defendants' Motion For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' Motion For Change Of Venue is OFF CALENDAR for department 501. This matter is set for hearing in Department 302. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Continued To Apr-03-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER LEICHUS AND MARLENE LEICHUS' MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is continued to April 3, 2015 to give all parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. All parties must deliver courtesy copies of the papers they filed to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 3, 2015 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Judge : Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/ehg
